pattern: Strip the separators, then regroup from the right so only the leading group may be short — a reversed scan with a running counter.
hints: The dashes already in the input carry no meaning; the only thing that matters is the sequence of alphanumeric characters and the group size k.
Because every group except the first must be exactly k long, it is the rightmost characters that align cleanly — think about walking the cleaned string from the end.
Drop all dashes and uppercase the rest, then insert a dash before every k-th character counted from the right; reverse back to get the answer.

edge_inputs.description: redundant dashes collapse, first group shorter than k
only dashes leaves an empty key
length is an exact multiple of k so no short leading group
k larger than the cleaned length yields a single group

stress.description: long random key with frequent dashes and mixed case
stress.note: may contain runs of dashes and an all-dash prefix/suffix
